full_text_rationale: Full text describes OpenSage with AI-created graph-based long-term memory managed by a dedicated memory agent, sharing high-level knowledge across tasks and evaluated on SWE-Bench Pro and other agent benchmarks.
extractor_notes: Fresh search architecture extraction batch 011; controlled-comparison and quality status remain pending aggregate recomputation.
autores: Hongwei Li, Zhun Wang, Qinrun Dai, Yuzhou Nie, Jinjun Peng, Ruitong Liu, Jingyang Zhang, Kaijie Zhu, Jingxuan He, Lun Wang, Yangruibo Ding, Yueqi Chen, Wenbo Guo, Dawn Song
formato_armazenamento: Hierarchical graph memory with execution-based short-term memory and target-level long-term memory in Neo4j. Short-term graph stores agent runs, events, tool calls, raw outputs, summaries, and cross-agent calls; long-term graph stores entities, code structures, user queries, answers, and relationships managed by a memory agent.
framework_agente: OpenSage agent development kit with AI-created topology, dynamic tools, hierarchical graph memory, and dedicated memory agent
llm_utilizado: GPT-5, GPT-5.3-Codex, Gemini 3 Flash, Gemini 3 Pro, GPT-5 Mini, Claude Opus 4.5, Claude Opus 4.6, Claude Sonnet 4
metodo_avaliacao: benchmark; leaderboard comparison; topology ablation; tooling ablation; memory ablation; cost-throughput comparison; LOCOMO appendix evaluation
benchmarks_utilizados: CyberGym, Terminal-Bench 2.0, SWE-Bench Pro Python, DevOps-Gym, LOCOMO appendix
metricas_desempenho: resolved rate, confidence intervals for Terminal-Bench, cost in Terminal-Bench collaboration analysis, memory-ablation resolved rate, LOCOMO LLM-as-judge scores
metricas_cl: Graph-based short-term and long-term memory, memory-agent storage/retrieval/update operations, NoMem and Mem0g ablations, examples of stored search results, code understanding, and environment errors.
baseline_comparado: Anthropic Agent, OpenHands, Droid, Simple Codex, Claude Code, SWE-agent, Agentless, Mem0g, NoMem, OpenSage feature ablations
tamanho_memoria_ao_longo_tempo: Memory grows as agents create graph nodes and edges for short-term execution events and long-term target knowledge; the paper does not report aggregate memory-entry counts.
custo_por_tarefa: NR; Terminal-Bench analysis reports a resolved-rate and cost tradeoff for large-small collaboration, but no per-task memory cost is isolated.
resultado_principal: SageAgent via OpenSage ranks first in the reported comparisons: 60.2% resolved on CyberGym, 78.4 +/- 2.2 on Terminal-Bench 2.0, 59.0% on SWE-Bench Pro, and 46.8% on DevOps-Gym.
desempenho_vs_baseline: On SWE-Bench Pro, SageAgent with Gemini 3 Flash reaches 59.0% versus SWE-agent 40.2% and Agentless 9.4%. In the memory ablation, OpenSage reaches 59.0 versus Mem0g 56.4 and NoMem 56.2.
custo_vs_baseline: The Terminal-Bench collaboration analysis reports that pairing Gemini 3 Pro for planning/review with GPT-5 Mini for execution matches GPT-5 performance while reducing cost compared with Gemini 3 Pro alone.
limitacoes: The paper notes that current SOTA models do not yet use OpenSage's advanced features consistently and may misuse them, indicating a model-capability gap for AI-centered agent construction.
ameacas_validade: Results depend on benchmark leaderboards, selected backbones, added task-specific code for SWE-Bench Pro, graph-memory schema/tool design, generated node types, memory-agent decisions, and whether ADK-level gains generalize beyond the evaluated environments.
